narrative.headline: 2022 Honda HR-V: electrical system leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 76 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 33%, followed by the engine (22%) and power train (20%). No deaths are reported. The brakes draw fewer complaints (18%) but the most crashes (5) and injuries (3) of any area, so they are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 25 reports (33%), with 2 crashes, 1 fire, and 2 injuries. Watch for warning lights and gear-selection problems.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 17 reports (22%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. Watch for loss of power while driving.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 15 reports (20%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. A recurring complaint area worth checking on a test drive.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Only 14 reports (18%) but the most serious by outcome: 5 crashes, 3 injuries, and 1 fire. Test the brakes thoroughly.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 12 reports (16%), linked to 1 crash. Watch for unexpected changes in speed while driving.
